storage_policy.copyright_boundary: Store only paraphrased observations and extracted design rules; do not store copied media, screenshots, transcripts, brand marks, or raw assets.

qa_gates: Confirm each source record stores derived rules only and no copied media, screenshots, transcripts, brand marks, layouts, or prose.
Confirm every source record has a contact sheet qa_probe and public_blocked release boundary before generation.

records.anchor: public launch article observed as a design-system-to-product narrative
records.visual_observation: The launch story becomes more persuasive when system breadth is reduced to a few high-contrast product surfaces instead of a catalogue.
records.transcript_or_teaching_claim: Paraphrased claim: explain the system as a workflow customers can adopt, not as disconnected feature announcements.
records.extracted_design_rule: For cover and setup roles, show one launch promise and one native system surface before introducing detailed proof.
records.native_ppt_implication: Generate native editable headline text, shape groups, and product-surface placeholders rather than copied imagery.
records.anti_copy_boundary: do not copy source visuals, source brand, screenshots, layouts, icons, or launch article prose.
records.qa_probe: The contact sheet should read as an original launch deck and not a disguised source page.

records.anchor: demo pacing observed as a climax beat without retaining frames
records.visual_observation: A demo climax works when attention converges on one object and supporting labels arrive after the object is understood.
records.transcript_or_teaching_claim: Paraphrased claim: spoken emphasis should become a short native label and a scale change, not a transcript block.
records.extracted_design_rule: For the climax role, reserve the largest field for one native proof object and keep supporting explanation secondary.
records.native_ppt_implication: Build the climax as native editable PPT shapes, text, and connectors with a traceable focal object.
records.anti_copy_boundary: do not copy video frames, audio, transcript text, product brand, demo UI, or source camera composition.
records.qa_probe: The contact sheet should show one dominant climax object before any label is read.

records.anchor: hierarchy tutorial observed for headline/body contrast
records.visual_observation: Slides become easier to scan when one headline, one proof phrase, and one secondary note have clearly different type roles.
records.transcript_or_teaching_claim: Paraphrased claim: visual hierarchy should guide attention through contrast, scale, and grouping.
records.extracted_design_rule: Use a deterministic editorial type ladder so setup, contrast, and proof slides do not collapse into equal-weight prose.
records.native_ppt_implication: Use native editable PPT text boxes with explicit type roles and traceable hierarchy decisions.
records.anti_copy_boundary: do not copy tutorial examples, source brand, screenshots, diagrams, lesson prose, or type specimens.
records.qa_probe: The contact sheet should reveal a readable hierarchy before body text is legible.

records.anchor: slide design teaching observed for whitespace and grid discipline
records.visual_observation: Editorial spacing makes proof feel designed when margins, gutters, and object scale visibly separate priority levels.
records.transcript_or_teaching_claim: Paraphrased claim: whitespace is an active organizer rather than unused space.
records.extracted_design_rule: For contrast and proof roles, use a grid with large margins, deliberate gutters, and a visibly dominant proof area.
records.native_ppt_implication: Construct spacing with native editable shape coordinates, margins, and guides that remain traceable in PPT.
records.anti_copy_boundary: do not copy source layouts, grids, course visuals, source brand, screenshots, or exercise files.
records.qa_probe: The contact sheet should show differentiated margins and proof zones, not equal dashboard cards.

records.anchor: demo rhythm observed from pacing only, with no media stored
records.visual_observation: A launch deck feels more deliberate when setup pauses, proof builds, climax expands, and close removes detail.
records.transcript_or_teaching_claim: Paraphrased claim: narration cadence can become a slide density and reveal budget.
records.extracted_design_rule: Map pacing to the six-slide rhythm so proof density rises only after the audience has a clear orientation beat.
records.native_ppt_implication: Record native editable reveal objects and trace the rhythm decision without embedding raw video or audio.
records.anti_copy_boundary: do not copy source video, audio, transcript, brand, frames, screenshots, or demo sequence.
records.qa_probe: The contact sheet should show a clear rhythm change from setup to proof to climax.

records.anchor: product surface interaction observed as decision flow
records.visual_observation: The close works when the product surface becomes a decision handoff rather than another feature tour.
records.transcript_or_teaching_claim: Paraphrased claim: interaction cues should clarify what the audience can do next.
records.extracted_design_rule: For close and proof roles, show the handoff as an original native flow with a release gate and trace fields.
records.native_ppt_implication: Represent interaction with native editable PPT steps, connectors, labels, and traceable workflow ids.
records.anti_copy_boundary: do not copy source UI, source brand, interaction screenshots, product names, icons, or layout patterns.
records.qa_probe: The contact sheet should show an original handoff flow with no source-brand resemblance.
